column1: # Talo deprecation registry — every form deliberately RETIRED and replaced, so a
# form-retirement is a recorded, gated act, not a silent change (the project's
# "decisions are constitutional, recorded not silent" discipline; docs/0000).
# Scope: deliberate, post-construction retirements. The early sourcing pivot
# (docs/0003) and the buffer-vowel/euphony mechanical passes reshaped many forms
# before any stable vocabulary existed; those are documented in the ADRs, not
# tracked per-form here. This registry begins with the docs/0011 §5.1 freeze
# checklist re-mints and grows as forms are retired going forward.
# The gate (tools/deprecation-registry) enforces:
#   1. no `old_form` is still LIVE (in lexicon/derived/compounds) — a retired
#      spelling must stay dead, so it can't silently come back for a new meaning;
#   2. each `new_form` IS live under its `id` — the recorded replacement is real;
#   3. each `id` still exists in the lexicon.
# Columns: old_form<TAB>new_form<TAB>id<TAB>gloss<TAB>reason<TAB>decision<TAB>date
# ('#' = comment). new_form may be '∅' for a retirement with no replacement.
old_form
tebana
